pends on the intellectual, moral and spiritual growth of the in
dividual. On the purely physical plane the rate of growth can
be a fixed unit per year'. But man is more than a physical
object. As such the rate of progression is bound to vary from
individual to individual; and it also varies from one period to
another in the life ofUte same individual. This appears to be
the truth underlying the method advocated by Mr. Robert
Y aget in his paper in the 1957 Annual Number of THE
ASTROLOGICAL MAGAZINE.
Most of the arcs of progression per year vary from 57
minutes to 65 minutes; and only a few go beyond or below
these figures. The arc may be obtained either from the rate in
which the Sun's apparent motion is found after birth; or one
may take recourse to the motion of the Moon, or to the sidereal
time. We have to enquire whether we can fix the approximate
measure of progression.
As we advance, we are carrying with us the zodiacal pat
tern found at the moment of our birth.
